text: Hello and welcome to Real Easy English.
zh: 大家好,欢迎收听《Real Easy English》。

text: In this podcast we have real conversations in Easy English to help you learn.
zh: 在这个播客节目里,我们用简单英语进行真实对话,帮助大家学习。

text: I'm Beth.
zh: 我是Beth。

text: And I'm Georgie.
zh: 我是Georgie。

text: You can find a video version of this podcast and a worksheet to help you learn on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到这个播客的视频版本和配套练习题,帮助你学习。

text: Hello Georgie, how are you today?
zh: 你好Georgie,今天过得怎么样?

text: I'm very well, thank you Beth.
zh: 我很好,谢谢你Beth。

text: How are you?
zh: 你呢?

text: I'm very good, very good.
zh: 我也很好,非常好。

text: Today we are talking about mess.
zh: 今天我们要聊聊“mess”这个话题。

text: Now mess is when something is untidy.
zh: mess指的是东西不整洁、乱糟糟的状态。

text: I left my house in a bit of a mess this morning.
zh: 我今天早上出门时家里有点乱。

text: Oh dear Beth, that's not very good.
zh: 哦天哪Beth,这可不太好。

text: I slept in a bit too long.
zh: 我今天早上睡过头了。

text: My kitchen is a mess.
zh: 我的厨房乱糟糟的。

text: There are pans on the side.
zh: 锅都堆在灶台边上。

text: I don't want to think about it really.
zh: 我都不想去想它了。

text: Beth, that sounds disgusting.
zh: Beth,这听起来太糟糕了。

text: Are you a messy person in general?
zh: 你平时是个邋遢的人吗?

text: I like things to be clean, but if there is a bit of mess, I'm okay with it, but I try to sort out the mess as soon as I can.
zh: 我喜欢东西干净整洁,但如果有点乱,我也能接受,不过我会尽快把它收拾好。

text: but you know how sometimes you cook dinner for example
zh: 不过你知道吗,比如有时候你做完晚饭,

text: and then I don't do the washing up immediately because I want to eat my food hot so I will eat relax for a bit and then I will clean the kitchen.
zh: 我不会马上洗碗,因为我想趁热吃饭,所以我会先放松一下,吃完之后再去打扫厨房。

text: Do you have like a rule in your house for the length of time you can leave the washing up like does it have to be that evening
zh: 你们家有没有规定,比如碗必须在多长时间内洗掉,一定要当晚洗吗?

text: or can it be the next day i like it to be that evening but it's not always that evening which i don't like what about you in your house
zh: 还是可以留到第二天?我比较喜欢当晚洗,但并不总能做到,这一点我不太喜欢。你家里是怎样的呢?

text: is it tidy is it messy it's usually quite tidy because i live with two other people so we all try to be considerate
zh: 是整洁还是凌乱?通常挺整洁的,因为我和另外两个人一起住,我们都会尽量为彼此考虑,

text: with the shared spaces so yeah and my room I try to tidy every few days so yeah.
zh: 对公共空间也是这样,所以还好。至于我自己的房间,我每隔几天就会打扫一次。

text: That sounds quite tidy.
zh: 听起来挺整洁的。

text: Beth do you have a favourite job to do around the house or a least favourite job?
zh: Beth,你在家务活里有没有最喜欢或者最不喜欢做的事?

text: I have a least favourite job and that is washing up.
zh: 我有一个最不喜欢做的家务,那就是洗碗。

text: I hate washing the dishes dishes.
zh: 我讨厌洗碗。

text: But we have a dishwasher so I use the dishwasher but there are some things
zh: 不过我们家有洗碗机,所以我一般用洗碗机,但有些东西,

text: like some pans that I should wash up so I sometimes do them but my husband does them quite a lot.
zh: 比如有些锅,是需要手洗的,我有时候会洗,但大多数时候是我丈夫在洗。

text: Is there any way that you make washing up a more enjoyable activity?
zh: 你有没有什么办法能让洗碗变得更有趣一点?

text: There is!
zh: 有的!

text: I put music on,
zh: 我会放点音乐,

text: I put a song on and I make sure I've finished by the end of the song and it
zh: 放一首歌,然后尽量在歌曲结束前把碗洗完,

text: just makes me happy because I can just sing along and not focus on hating washing up and it's not even that bad.
zh: 这样就会让我开心一点,因为我可以跟着唱歌,而不是老想着讨厌洗碗这件事,而且其实也没有那么糟糕。

text: I quite like tidying my room because I find it quite satisfying because
zh: 我还挺喜欢整理自己的房间的,因为我觉得挺有成就感的,

text: you you end up with a good result at the end you have a tidy space to relax in and spend time in that's actually on my to -do list at the moment.
zh: 因为最后你会得到一个整洁的成果,有一个整洁的空间可以放松、待着,其实这现在正好也在我的待办事项清单上。

text: Later I'm going to go home and tidy my room.
zh: 等一下我要回家整理房间。

text: Beth, do you have anything on your to -do list?
zh: Beth,你的待办清单上有什么事吗?

text: I do need to hoover.
zh: 我确实需要吸尘。

text: So hoovering is on my to -do list.
zh: 所以吸地是我待办清单上的一项。

text: A to -do list is a list of jobs that you need to do.
zh: 待办清单就是记录你需要完成的事情的清单。

text: So I do need to hoover but I'm not sure if I'll do it today.
zh: 所以我确实需要吸尘,但我不确定今天会不会做。

text: I might do it tomorrow.
zh: 可能会拖到明天。

text: Okay, let's recap the vocabulary we heard during the conversation, starting with mess.
zh: 好了,我们来复习一下这次对话中出现的词汇,先从mess开始。

text: A mess is when something is untidy or dirty.
zh: mess指的是某样东西又脏又乱的状态。

text: For example, this morning I left my kitchen in a bit of a mess.
zh: 比如说,今天早上我把厨房弄得乱糟糟的。

text: We also use the adjective messy to describe a dirty or untidy place.
zh: 我们也会用形容词messy来形容一个又脏又乱的地方。

text: We can also describe a person as messy, which means that they don't clean things and they often make a mess.
zh: 我们也可以用messy来形容一个人,意思是这个人不太爱收拾,经常把地方弄乱。

text: The opposite of messy is tidy.
zh: messy的反义词是tidy(整洁的)。

text: So a place like your bedroom or Georgie's bedroom can be tidy.
zh: 所以像你的卧室或者Georgie的卧室这样的地方可以说是tidy。

text: And you can also be described as a tidy person.
zh: 一个人也可以被形容为tidy person(爱整洁的人)。

text: That's if you usually clean things and don't make a mess.
zh: 也就是说这个人通常会把东西收拾干净,不会弄乱。

text: And a to -do list is a list of jobs you need to do.
zh: 而to-do list就是你需要完成的事情清单。

text: Sometimes jobs around the house, like cleaning.
zh: 有时候是家务活,比如打扫卫生。

text: That's it for this episode of Real Easy English.
zh: 这就是本期《Real Easy English》的全部内容。

text: If you've enjoyed this episode about mess and want to learn more vocabulary for cleaning, Georgie has made a video, Phrasal Verbs for Housework.
zh: 如果你喜欢这期关于mess的节目,还想学习更多关于打扫卫生的词汇,Georgie做了一个视频,叫《家务相关的短语动词》。

text: Find it on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到。

text: And don't forget to try the worksheet on our website to test what you've learnt.
zh: 别忘了到我们网站上做练习题,检验一下你学到的内容。

text: We'll be back next week with another conversation in Easy English.
zh: 我们下周将带来另一段简单英语对话,再见面。

text: See you then.
zh: 到时候见。

text: Bye!
zh: 拜拜!
